*Eumenes fraternus* is a species of potter wasp native to the eastern United States and Canada. It is black with ivory markings and measures 15–20 mm (0.6–0.8 in) in length. Females construct mud nests, provisioning them with paralyzed caterpillars for their larvae. Adults feed on nectar and pollen. The species inhabits glades, shrublands, and forest edges, active from spring to late fall.
Wilhelm von Henke (1834–1896) was a German anatomist and professor, known for his work on "Henke's space" and theories about the Venus de Milo's asymmetry.

USCGC Robert Yered (WPC-1104) is a Sentinel-class cutter of the United States Coast Guard, launched on March 2, 2012, and commissioned on February 15, 2013. Named after Engineman First Class Robert J. Yered, the vessel is designed for coastal security, drug interdiction, and search and rescue operations, and is currently in active service.
Joseph Buck is an American soccer midfielder currently playing for the Georgetown Hoyas, having previously played for New England Revolution II.

Margaret JoBeth Williams (born December 6, 1948) is an American actress and television director. Born in Houston, Texas, she began her career with a role on the children's television series *Jabberwocky*. She gained recognition for her roles in motion pictures such as *Stir Crazy* (1980), *Poltergeist* (1982), and *The Big Chill* (1983).

Williams has also been lauded for her performances in television movies, including *The Day After* (1983) and *My Name is Bill W.* (1989), earning Emmy nominations for her roles in *Adam* (1983) and *Baby M* (1988). In 1995, she received an Academy Award nomination for directing the short film *On Hope*. She has also had recurring roles in television series such as *Private Practice*.

Williams is married to director John Pasquin, and they have two children.

Sir Robert James Buckland KBE KC MP (born 22 September 1968) is a British Conservative Party politician, barrister, and recorder. He has served as the Member of Parliament (MP) for South Swindon since 2010.

Buckland was appointed Solicitor General for England and Wales in 2014. In 2019, he became Minister of State for Prisons, and later that year, he was appointed Secretary of State for Justice and Lord Chancellor by Prime Minister Boris Johnson. In July 2022, he became Secretary of State for Wales under Johnson, and was re-appointed by Liz Truss. He returned to the backbenches in October 2022.

Celanese Corporation is a global leader in the chemicals and advanced materials industry, founded in 1918 by Swiss chemist Camille Dreyfus in New York City. Headquartered in Irving, Texas, the company specializes in producing a wide range of products, including acetyl intermediates, advanced engineered materials, and consumer specialties. Celanese is particularly known for its cellulose acetate, used in applications such as cigarette filters and fashion apparel. The company has undergone significant transformations, including mergers and acquisitions, and is publicly traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol "CE." As of 2021, Celanese reported revenues of $8.54 billion and employed over 8,500 people.

"Tsunagu" is a pop rock single by Japanese boy band Arashi, released on June 28, 2017, as part of their album "Untitled." The song debuted at number one on both the Oricon weekly singles chart and Billboard Japan's top single sales chart, selling over 389,000 copies in its first week. Used as the theme song for the film "Shinobi no Kuni," the single was certified platinum by RIAJ for shipments of 250,000 units.
Gerhard vom Rath (1830–1888) was a German geologist and mineralogist known for his contributions to mineralogy and crystallography.

Radio Dabanga is a Sudanese media organization broadcasting from Amsterdam, Netherlands. Established on December 1, 2008, it is owned and operated by Free Press Unlimited. Initially named Radio Darfur, the station primarily broadcasts in Sudanese Arabic, with past broadcasts in Darfuri Arabic, Fur, Masalit, and Zaghawa.

Radio Dabanga delivers news and programs via shortwave radio, satellite TV (since February 18, 2018), and online platforms. Shortwave broadcasts include morning and evening news bulletins, along with diverse programs addressing Sudanese issues. The TV satellite channel rebroadcasts radio programs 24/7. Online, Radio Dabanga publishes news articles in Arabic and English.

Reporting from exile, Radio Dabanga focuses on providing independent news and information to people across Sudan. The station relies on a network of reporters, stringers, and audience participation to gather and verify news. Despite government censorship attempts, Radio Dabanga reaches millions of listeners and viewers daily, offering a platform for voices often unheard. The station has faced controversies and blocking attempts by the Sudanese government, but continues to broadcast via Eutelsat.

Zheng Zhi (born August 20, 1980) is a Chinese professional footballer and manager, currently playing as a midfielder for Guangzhou F.C. and serving as the club's caretaker manager. He began his career in 1990 with Liaoning youth academies and made his professional debut in 1998. Zheng has played for several clubs, including Shenzhen Jianlibao, Shandong Luneng Taishan, Charlton Athletic, Celtic, and Guangzhou Evergrande, where he achieved significant success, including winning the AFC Champions League in 2013 and being named the Asian Footballer of the Year the same year. Internationally, Zheng represented China from 2002 to 2019, earning over 100 caps and captaining the national team during his tenure. He also led the under-23 team at the 2008 Summer Olympics. After transitioning to management, he took on caretaker roles at Guangzhou Evergrande and Guangzhou, further solidifying his impact on Chinese football.

The Highwomen is the debut studio album by the American country supergroup The Highwomen, released on September 6, 2019. Produced by Dave Cobb, the album features themes of female empowerment and representation in country music, with notable singles including "Redesigning Women" and "Crowded Table."

The 2014 FedEx Cup Playoffs were a series of four PGA Tour golf tournaments held from August 21 to September 14, 2014, concluding with the Tour Championship. Billy Horschel won the FedEx Cup after victories at the BMW Championship and Tour Championship, securing the $10 million bonus. The playoffs included The Barclays, Deutsche Bank Championship, BMW Championship, and Tour Championship, with fields narrowing from 125 to 30 players.

Anthony Di Pietro (born 15 February 1969) is an Australian businessman and chairman of Melbourne Victory FC since 2011. As CEO of Premier Fresh Australia, he has advocated for expanding Australia's food trade in Asia. Under his leadership, Melbourne Victory won multiple A-League titles and hosted high-profile events, including a record-breaking match against Liverpool FC in 2013.
Felix Johannes Oinas (1893-2004) was an Estonian folklorist and linguist who emigrated to the United States after World War II.

The C. H. Moore House, located at 219 E. Woodlawn St., Clinton, Illinois, was built in 1865 by John and Minerva Moore Bishop and later expanded in 1887. It served as the residence of attorney Clifton H. Moore, who owned a vast book collection. After Moore's death in 1901, the house was restored in 1967 by the DeWitt County Museum Association and now houses the C. H. Moore Homestead DeWitt County Museum, which features the mansion, gardens, and various historical exhibits. The house is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.

USS *Housatonic* (SP-1697) was a minelayer and freighter that served in the United States Navy during World War I. Originally built in 1899 as the merchant ship *El Rio*, she was acquired by the Navy in 1917 and converted into a minelayer. Commissioned in 1918, *Housatonic* laid over 9,300 mines in the North Sea as part of the North Sea Mine Barrage. After the war, she transported American troops home before being decommissioned in 1919 and returned to civilian service. Renamed *Brazos* in 1925, she operated as a freighter until sinking in a collision with HMS *Archer* in 1942.

Robert N. Bellah (February 23, 1927 – July 30, 2013) was an American sociologist and influential scholar of religion, best known for coining the term "American civil religion" and for his works *Habits of the Heart* (1985) and *Religion in Human Evolution* (2011). A Harvard graduate and professor at the University of California, Berkeley, Bellah explored the intersection of religion, morality, and society, earning numerous accolades, including the National Humanities Medal. His career was marked by significant contributions to the sociology of religion and communitarian thought.
Luise Koch (1860–1934) was a German politician, suffragist, and educator, known for her leadership in women's suffrage movements in Bremen.

Phebalium nottii, commonly known as Pink phebalium, is a shrub growing to 1–3 m in height, with pink to deep mauve flowers arranged in umbels. It was first described as Eriostemon nottii by Ferdinand von Mueller in 1867 and later renamed Phebalium nottii by Joseph Maiden and Ernst Betche in 1899. Found in inland Queensland and parts of New South Wales, it grows on sandstone in forest habitats.

The Wrocław Global Forum, established in 2010, is an annual event aimed at strengthening transatlantic dialogue to address global challenges. Held in Wrocław, Poland, it brings together politicians, diplomats, academics, business leaders, and activists to discuss issues such as energy security, NATO, and European strategy. The forum features high-profile speakers and presentations, with funding from both Polish and U.S. governments and corporate sponsors.

Augustin Schubert (born František; 1902 – July 28, 1942) was a Czech Augustinian priest known for his opposition to Nazism during the German occupation of Czechoslovakia. Born in Žižkov, Prague, he was fluent in both Czech and German and became a popular preacher. Schubert actively opposed the Nazi regime, delivering a poignant sermon on the day of the occupation. He was arrested by the Gestapo in 1940 and subsequently imprisoned in various concentration camps, where he suffered severe mistreatment. Schubert died in Dachau at the age of 40 and is currently undergoing the process of beatification.

Douglas Keszler is an American chemist and distinguished professor at Oregon State University, known for his research in inorganic and materials chemistry. His work includes developing hydrothermal dehydration techniques for solid synthesis and advancing amorphous oxide semiconductors for electronic displays. He earned his Ph.D. from Northwestern University in 1984 and has since contributed to innovations in thin-film transistors and aqueous-based nanomaterial synthesis.

Jack Ross is an academic and writer from Auckland, specializing in academic and creative writing at Massey University. Known for his contributions to experimental writing in New Zealand, Ross has edited several literary magazines and is recognized within avant-garde literary circles, though his work has received mixed critical reception.

*Baaji* (Urdu: باجی) is a 2019 Pakistani social drama film directed by Saqib Malik and Shah Fahad, and written by Irfan Ahmed Urfi. The film stars Meera, Osman Khalid Butt, Amna Ilyas, and Mohsin Abbas Haider. Set against the backdrop of the fading Lollywood film industry, *Baaji* explores themes of ambition, betrayal, and the struggle for relevance.

Produced by Page 33 Films and distributed by ARY Films, *Baaji* was released nationwide on June 28, 2019, and internationally by B4U Films. A teaser trailer was released in April 2019, followed by a theatrical trailer in May. The film generated significant buzz, further amplified by the release of a controversial song.

*Baaji* was a commercial success, grossing Rs. 120 million domestically against a budget of Rs. 60 million. It received generally positive reviews, with critics praising the performances of the cast, particularly Meera and Amna Ilyas. The film was later made available for streaming on Amazon Prime Video and had its television premiere on ARY Digital on Eid al-Fitr, May 26, 2020.
Nashipur is a village in West Bengal, India, known for its historical Rajbari and cultural events like Jhulan Yatra.

Gilad Efrat (born 1969) is an Israeli painter known for his layered, abstract landscapes and portraits. A professor at Shenkar College, his work explores memory, geopolitics, and the interplay between photography and painting.

Edith Guerrier (1870–1958) was an American librarian and social reformer. She co-founded the Saturday Evening Girls, a reading club for immigrant girls in Boston, and helped establish the Paul Revere Pottery, which provided vocational training. A pioneer in library services, she also worked with Herbert Hoover's Food Administration during World War I.
Beverly Williams is a pioneering African American television news anchor and reporter.

Pinstripe is a puzzle-adventure game developed and published by Atmos Games, released in 2017 for multiple platforms. Following Teddy, a disgraced ex-minister, the game involves rescuing his kidnapped daughter Bo from the shadowy figure Pinstripe. Created by Thomas Brush, the game received mixed reviews, with some praising its atmospheric depiction of hell and emotional depth, while others criticized its narrative and puzzle design.

Arnold V was the Count of Loon and Chiny. He fought in the Battle of Worringen and the War of Awans and Waroux. He signed the Peace of Fexhe. Towards the end of his reign, he sold the counties of Chiny and Loon to his son Louis. He died August 22, 1327.

Ida S. Baker High School is a public high school in Cape Coral, Florida, serving grades 9–12. Established in 2004, it is part of the Lee County School District and named after Ida S. Baker, the first Black female high school principal in Lee County. The school offers programs in business, engineering, and ROTC.

The Fruitmarket Gallery, established in 1974 in Edinburgh, Scotland, is a contemporary art space housed in a former fruit and vegetable market. Following a significant refurbishment completed in 2021, it features expanded exhibition areas, a café, and a bookshop. The gallery showcases works by both international and Scottish artists and offers free admission.

Pudliszki is a Polish food company headquartered in Pudliszki, Poland. Founded in 1920, the company produces fruit and vegetable products, including its well-known ketchup. Historically, the company exported goods to Germany, France, and the United States. Since 1997, the majority of Pudliszki's shares have been owned by Heinz.

Kreis Schwetz was a district in West Prussia from 1818 to 1920, with its capital in Schwetz. Established after Prussia's 1772 annexation of the region, it was ceded to Poland in 1920 under the Treaty of Versailles. Briefly reincorporated into Nazi Germany (1939–1945), it was restored to Poland after World War II. The district had a mixed German-Polish population.

Law & Order: Special Victims Unit (season 8) premiered on NBC on September 19, 2006, and concluded on May 22, 2007, with 22 episodes. The season starred Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay, Richard Belzer, Ice-T, Diane Neal, BD Wong, Tamara Tunie, and Dann Florek. Notable guest stars included Connie Nielsen, Chris "Ludacris" Bridges, Leslie Caron (who won an Emmy), and Jerry Lewis. The season introduced Adam Beach as Detective Chester Lake and featured storylines involving Olivia Benson's half-brother (played by Michael Weston).

Longwood House is a historic residence on Saint Helena where Napoleon lived in exile from 1815 until his death in 1821. Originally a farmhouse, it was later transferred to France in 1858. Now a museum, it is maintained by the French government as a memorial to Napoleon.

The Universal Time-Sharing System (UTS) was an operating system developed by Xerox Data Systems, first released in 1966 and discontinued thereafter. It was designed for the Sigma series of computers, including the Sigma 6, Sigma 7, and Sigma 9, and featured a command-line interface. UTS was succeeded by the CP-V operating system in 1973, which offered enhanced capabilities such as timesharing and remote processing. Other related systems included CP-R, a real-time operating system for Xerox 550 and Sigma 9 systems, and CP-6, a work-alike developed by Honeywell to support former UTS users.
François Picquet (December 4, 1708 – 1781) was a French Sulpician missionary who worked in New France, notably founding Fort de La Présentation in 1749.

"Me or Sum" is a hip hop and trap single by Nardo Wick featuring Lil Baby and Future, released on November 29, 2021. The song, from Nardo Wick's album Who Is Nardo Wick?, showcases the luxurious lifestyles and romantic conquests of the artists. An official music video, directed by MadeByJames and set in the Roaring Twenties, depicts scenes of gambling, heists, and romantic encounters.

Horace Edward Dobbs (born 14 January 1933) is a British scientist, researcher, and author known for his work with dolphins. He worked at the United Kingdom Atomic Energy Authority and founded International Dolphin Watch. Dobbs is an expert on dolphin behavior and opposes keeping them in captivity, believing swimming with dolphins has therapeutic effects.
Claude Frédéric t'Serclaes, Count of Tilly (1648–1723) was a field marshal in the Dutch States Army.

Waiau is a town in the Hurunui District of Canterbury, New Zealand, located on State Highway 70. As of the 2018 census, it has a population of 255. Historically significant, Waiau was the terminus of the Waiau Branch railway from 1919 to 1978 and was affected by the 2016 Kaikoura earthquake, which caused extensive damage to local infrastructure. The town features a primary school and a community swimming pool, and it is known for the Waiau Lodge Hotel, which was destroyed by fire in 2021.

Kenny Francis (born December 1, 1969) is an American NASCAR crew chief. He began racing go-karts at age eight, winning a state championship. Francis began working as a crew chief in the NASCAR Cup Series in 2003, winning 17 races and 21 poles. He won the 1999 NASCAR Winston Cup Series championship working for Robert Yates Racing.

The Luigi di Savoia Duca degli Abruzzi was an Italian cruiser of the Duca degli Abruzzi class, commissioned in 1937 and decommissioned in 1961. Built to be better protected and more heavily armed than its predecessors, it saw action during World War II, including internment after the Italian Armistice. Post-war, it underwent refits and served until 1972 when it was scrapped.

Nicole Bilderback (born June 10, 1975) is a South Korean-born American actress known for her roles in film and television. Adopted by American parents, she was raised in Tulsa, Oklahoma, and Dallas, Texas, where she began studying dance and acting at a young age. Bilderback gained recognition for her role in the 1995 film *Clueless* and its television adaptation. She has appeared in various other projects, including a notable role in the series *House* and the 2014 action film *Mercenaries*.

Serbs in Germany number over 241,000 Serbian nationals and 304,741 with Serbian ancestry. Concentrated primarily in North Rhine-Westphalia, Baden-Württemberg, and Bavaria, particularly in cities like Düsseldorf, Stuttgart, and Munich, they speak German and Serbian and predominantly follow the Serbian Orthodox Church.

Sharp Gallaher Racing, owned by Eddie Sharp and Bill Gallaher, operated in the Camping World Truck Series from 2010 to 2013. The team debuted in 2010 with Justin Lofton, who won the championship that year. Key drivers included Max Gresham, who ran the full 2013 season. The team relocated to Mooresville, North Carolina, in 2013, but closed down later that year.

Braille pattern dots-1456 is used in unified international braille to represent the voiceless dental fricative, /θ/. Related patterns using dots 7 and 8 are used in 8-dot braille systems. In Japanese kantenji braille, related patterns are 2568, 12568, 24568, and 124568, with additional dots placed above the base 6-dot cell.

Bethlehem Atlantic Works, originally comprising the East Boston Dry Dock Company and Simpson Dry Dock Company, began operations in 1853 and became a major ship repair facility for the U.S. Navy during World War II. Employing thousands of workers, it was the largest private ship repair facility in Boston for over a century. The shipyard faced financial difficulties in the 1950s and closed in 1951. Racial tensions peaked in the late 1970s and early 1980s, leading to the Navy's abrupt departure in 1984. The land was later auctioned to Massachusetts Port Authority for development, with much of the original site now hosting residential and recreational facilities.

Bilston is a village in Midlothian, Scotland, with a population of 1,440 (2022), located near the Bilston Glen Colliery site and the River Forth. Known for its historical coal mining and a Site of Special Scientific Interest, Bilston features Dryden Tower and Bilston Primary School. A proposed bypass faced significant opposition, leading to a long-running protest camp that protected the SSSI.
Ruth Cameron was a singer, producer, and Charlie Haden's manager.

Raquel Torres Cerdán (born November 19, 1948, in Xalapa) is a Mexican chef and anthropologist known for her dedication to preserving indigenous Veracruz cuisine. She has opened two restaurants and teaches workshops on traditional Mexican cooking.

Bernie Vince (born 2 October 1985 in Stansbury, South Australia) is a former Australian Rules football player who played as a midfielder for Adelaide (2006–2013) and Melbourne (2014–2018). He won the Malcolm Blight Medallist in 2009, the Keith 'Bluey' Truscott Medal in 2015, and the Michael Tuck Medallist in 2012. Vince began his career with Woodville-West Torrens and was drafted by Adelaide in 2005. He later joined Melbourne, where he won the club's best and fairest award. Off the field, he married Abbie Noonan in 2017 and has one son.

The Finse Tunnel (Norwegian: Finsetunnelen) is a 10.3-kilometer (6.4 mi) railway tunnel on the Bergen Line in Norway, located near Finse. Situated at an elevation of 1,237 meters (4,058 ft) above sea level, it is the highest point on the Norwegian railway network. The single-track tunnel opened on May 16, 1993, replacing a challenging section of track frequently blocked by snow.

The decision to build the tunnel arose from persistent operational disruptions and high costs associated with maintaining the Bergen Line through the Finse area. Construction began in 1990, and upon completion, the tunnel reduced the line length by 4.5 kilometers (2.8 mi) and travel time by 8–10 minutes. The project also included upgrades to the adjacent line, allowing for increased speeds of up to 170 kilometers per hour (110 mph). While the tunnel has improved the reliability of rail transport, safety concerns have been raised regarding emergency access and evacuation procedures in the event of a fire.

The 2nd Infantry Brigade was a military formation of the New Zealand Military Forces, active from 1916 to 1919 and again from 1924 to 1964. Initially formed in Egypt for service on the Western Front during World War I, it participated in significant battles such as Broodseinde, Messines, and Passchendaele. The brigade was disbanded in 1919 after the war. A subsequent Territorial Force formation retained the 2nd Infantry Brigade designation until its disbandment in 1964.

A composite film is a cinematic work composed of interconnected but self-contained stories, unified by recurring elements such as themes, characters, or settings. Examples include *Pulp Fiction*, *Love Actually*, and *Paris, je t'aime*. These films may use titled segments, varied sequencing, and distinct or unified musical scores to enhance cohesion. The structure draws from literary traditions like composite novels and short story cycles, emphasizing both individual narratives and their collective impact.

Bristol is a city in Sullivan County, Tennessee, United States. The population was 27,147 at the 2020 census. It is the twin city of Bristol, Virginia, which lies directly across the state line.

Bristol is known as the "Birthplace of Country Music," a designation formalized by the U.S. Congress in 1998, due to the historic 1927 Bristol sessions. These recording sessions, organized by Ralph Peer of Victor Records, captured early performances by the Carter Family and Jimmie Rodgers, marking a pivotal moment in the development of country music. The Birthplace of Country Music Museum commemorates this legacy. A music festival called the Bristol Rhythm & Roots Reunion takes place every year during the third weekend in September.

Bristol is the home of the Bristol Motor Speedway, a NASCAR venue, and Bristol Dragway, which hosts NHRA events. Bristol Tennessee City Schools operates the public schools for almost all of the city.

Petro Pysarchuk (born June 6, 1955) is a Ukrainian politician and entrepreneur who served as a Deputy in the Ukrainian Verhovna Rada from 2002 to 2007 under the Party of Regions. He has held various positions including CEO of small enterprises and general manager of AJPIE-L Co. Ltd., and is known for founding the largest trade complex in Lviv.

"Me or Sum" is a hip hop and trap single by Nardo Wick featuring Lil Baby and Future, released on November 29, 2021, as part of Nardo Wick's debut album *Who Is Nardo Wick?*. The song explores themes of luxury, relationships, and romantic allure, with each artist delivering verses reflecting their lifestyles. The accompanying music video, released on February 3, 2022, is set in the Roaring Twenties and depicts a heist planned and executed by the rappers, directed by MadeByJames.
George Hall (September 21, 1795 – April 16, 1868) was an American politician who served as the first mayor of Brooklyn from 1834 to 1835.

The Rachel Carson Center for Environment and Society is a research institution dedicated to exploring the interactions between human agents and nature, emphasizing the role of the humanities in environmental debates. It hosts approximately ten "Carson Fellows," who are post-doctoral researchers and established scholars from diverse backgrounds. The center focuses on six thematic research clusters, including Natural Disasters and Cultures of Risk, Resource Use and Conservation, and Environmental Ethics, Politics, and Movements. Additionally, it organizes public events, offers a doctoral program, and publishes various academic works.

Mass spectrometry imaging (MSI) is an analytical technique that visualizes the spatial distribution of molecules within a sample by combining mass spectrometry with imaging capabilities. It was first developed using secondary ion mass spectrometry (SIMS) in the 1960s but gained widespread use in the late 1990s with matrix-assisted laser desorption/ionization (MALDI), enabling the study of biomolecules in tissues. MSI operates through two main approaches: microprobe (scanning a focused ion beam to build spectra point-by-point) and microscope (directly imaging ion distributions with a position-sensitive detector). Common ionization methods include SIMS (high resolution, small areas), MALDI (suitable for large biomolecules), and desorption electrospray ionization (DESI) (minimal sample preparation, broad applications).  

MSI is used in clinical research, pharmacology, and biomarker discovery, allowing the study of molecular changes in diseases like cancer. It can be combined with other imaging techniques, such as fluorescence microscopy and MRI, for enhanced analysis. Standardized data formats like imzML facilitate interoperability between instruments and software. Despite its advantages—high selectivity and molecular specificity—challenges include resolution limitations and complex data analysis. Free software tools (e.g., MSiReader, Cardinal, SPUTNIK) support data processing and visualization.

Sage Kotsenburg (born July 27, 1993) is an American professional snowboarder specializing in slopestyle and big air. He won gold in slopestyle at the 2014 Winter Olympics in Sochi, becoming the event's first Olympic champion and the first U.S. medalist of those Games. A three-time Winter X Games medalist, Kotsenburg is known for innovative tricks, including the "Holy Crail" grab. Born in Coeur d'Alene, Idaho, he grew up in Park City, Utah, and turned professional in 2010.

Lu Rongjing (born August 1933) is a Chinese politician who served as Communist Party Secretary of Anhui (1988–1996) and Governor of Anhui (1988–1989). He later chaired the Anhui Provincial Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (1996–2000). Beginning his career in the Tongguanshan Copper Mine, Lu rose through provincial leadership roles, including deputy party secretary of Tongling and Ma'anshan, before assuming top positions in Anhui's government and party apparatus.

Madden NFL 25, released in 2013, is the 25th installment in the popular Madden NFL series developed by EA Tiburon and published by EA Sports. Featuring Hall of Fame running back Barry Sanders on the cover, the game introduced several improvements, including enhanced graphics, better real-time physics, and a new running control system. Notable additions include the ability to relocate NFL teams to international cities and the introduction of the "Run Free" feature, which allows players to think more like a quarterback. The game was released for various platforms, including PlayStation 3, Xbox 360, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, iOS, and Android, with specific release dates for each.

Deskaheh (March 15, 1873 – June 27, 1925), born Levi General, was a Cayuga hereditary chief and statesman of the Six Nations of the Grand River. As a prominent Indigenous rights advocate, he petitioned the League of Nations in the 1920s to challenge Canadian policies, seeking international recognition of Haudenosaunee sovereignty. His efforts, though unsuccessful, made him a key figure in Indigenous resistance.
James Atwell (1946–2020) was a British Anglican priest who served as Dean of Winchester from 2006 to 2016.

On February 20, 2015, at 06:43 CET, an Interregio train collided with an S-Bahn train in Rafz, Switzerland, causing partial derailment and six injuries. The incident occurred on the Eglisau–Neuhausen railway line and is under investigation for potential safety equipment failures.

*Riffs* is a live album by alto saxophonist Jimmy Lyons, released in 1982. It was recorded at Le Dreher club in Paris on September 13–14, 1980, and features Lyons with bassoonist Karen Borca, bassist Jay Oliver, and drummer Paul Murphy. The album contains lengthy versions of "Theme" and "Riffs #1/II".

Sir George Lockhart (1673 – December 17, 1731) was a Scottish politician and member of the Tory party, known for his opposition to the Treaty of Union between Scotland and England. He served as a Member of Parliament for Midlothian from 1708 to 1715 and briefly for Wigtown Burghs in 1708. Lockhart was a prominent figure in the Jacobite movement, participating in the 1715 uprising and later escaping to the Continent to avoid arrest. He authored significant historical writings, including "Memoirs of the Affairs of Scotland," which provide insight into Jacobite history.

"The Crocodile" is the fourth episode of the second season of *Once Upon a Time*. It explores the backstory of Rumplestiltskin (Robert Carlyle) and his feud with Captain Hook (Colin O'Donoghue), revealing how Hook lost his hand and earned his name. In Storybrooke, Belle (Emilie de Ravin) confronts Mr. Gold about his secrets while reopening the town library. Meanwhile, in the Enchanted Forest, Hook allies with Cora (Barbara Hershey) to reach Storybrooke. The episode received positive reviews for its action and character development.
Waingaro is a rural community in the Waikato District of New Zealand. At the 2018 census, it had a population of 180. Waingaro is known for its hot springs and Waingaro Landing.

Stadtallendorf is a town in the Marburg-Biedenkopf district of Hesse, Germany. It is known for being a secret munitions center during World War II and for its successful economy. The population was 21,552 on December 31, 2022.

Dimitar Dobrev (February 12, 1868 – April 11, 1944) was a Bulgarian naval officer who served in the Bulgarian and Russian navies from 1885 to 1914. He participated in several significant conflicts, including the Serbo-Bulgarian War, Russo-Japanese War, and the Balkan Wars. Dobrev held various positions, including commander of the cruiser Nadezhda and head of mobile defense during the First Balkan War. He was recognized for his bravery with multiple awards, including the Bulgarian Order of Saint Alexander and the Russian Order of St. Vladimir. After leaving the navy, he worked as a lawyer in Veliko Tarnovo. A ship in the Bulgarian Navy is named in his honor.

The Irish Centre for High-End Computing (ICHEC) is a national high-performance computing centre in Ireland, established in 2005. It operates supercomputers such as Kay, named after Kathleen Antonelli, and provides services to researchers, government agencies, and industry.

Aloysius Edward "Pie" Piechota (January 19, 1914 – June 13, 1996) was a professional baseball player whose career spanned 15 seasons, two of which were spent with the Major League Baseball (MLB) Boston Bees / Braves from 1940 to 1941. Piechota, a pitcher, compiled an earned run average (ERA) of 5.66, allowing 39 earned runs off of 68 hits, 6 home runs, and 42 walks while recording 18 strikeouts over 62 innings pitched. Piechota also played in 14 seasons of minor league baseball. He made his MLB debut at the age of 26 and was officially listed as standing 6 feet 0 inches (183 cm) and weighing 195 pounds (88 kg).

Benton Township is one of ten townships in Adair County, Missouri, United States. As of the 2010 census, its population was 19,700. It is named for Thomas Hart Benton, one of the inaugural pair of U.S. senators from Missouri.

'(born 1973) is a German political scientist, lawyer and politician of the Christian Democratic Union of Germany (CDU). A resident of Belgium, he is an official of the Secretariat of the European Parliament. As a political scientist, he is known as a student of Eckhard Jesse in the tradition of German extremism research, and has published works on the political tradition of the former Party of Democratic Socialism. He was one of the leading figures within the Junge Union during the early 2000s and is currently chairman of the Brussels branch of the CDU, the party's only branch outside Germany. He ran unsuccessfully as a CDU candidate for the Bundestag in the 2005 German federal election.

Owain Sebastian Yeoman is a Welsh actor. His credits include The Nine, Kitchen Confidential, AMC's Turn (as Benedict Arnold) and the HBO series Generation Kill. Additionally, he portrayed CBI Agent Wayne Rigsby in The Mentalist and portrayed Benny Gallagher in Emergence.

Alexander Ignatyevich Tarasov-Rodionov (Lang-ru; October 7, 1885 – September 3, 1938) was a Russian/Soviet writer and revolutionary, best known for his novel "Chocolate" which at the time of publication was acclaimed as a tale of heroic self-sacrifice but has since been criticized as a justification for the Red Terror.

The Gateway, also known as Curtains, is a 2015 horror film that was directed by Jaron Henrie-McCrea. The film had its world premiere on August 31, 2015 at Film4 FrightFest and stars Danni Smith and Tim Lueke as two activists that investigate a series of disappearing shower curtains in Smith's apartment.

Vinita Marwaha Madill (born 9 January 1987) is a British Space Operations engineer and science communicator. She is based at the European Space Agency through TERMA B.V. in the Netherlands, where she is responsible for the development of the European Robotic Arm. She founded the platform Rocket Women, which uses stories and interviews to encourage women to study STEM.
General Applied Science Laboratory (GASL) is an American aerospace company, known as a pioneer of hypersonic propulsion.
Richard Jata (born September 21, 1985 in Dunedin, Florida) is an American soccer player.

Mongolia competed at the 2004 Summer Olympics in Athens, Greece, from 13 to 29 August 2004. This was the nation's tenth appearance at the Olympics, except the 1984 Summer Olympics in Los Angeles, because of its partial support to the Soviet boycott.

The Mongolian National Olympic Committee sent a total of 20 athletes, 13 men and 7 women, to compete in 7 different sports, tying its delegation count with Sydney four years earlier. In the wake of Munkhbayar Dorjsuren's transfer to the German team, three Mongolian athletes had previously competed in Sydney, including shooter Otryadyn Gündegmaa in the women's pistol events. Judoka and world champion Damdinsürengiin Nyamkhüü was appointed by the committee to become Mongolia's flag bearer in the opening ceremony, dressed in a traditional costume.

After failing to achieve a single Olympic medal from Sydney, Mongolia left Athens with only a bronze from twenty-year-old judoka Khashbaataryn Tsagaanbaatar on the first day of the Games.

The 47th Walker Cup Match was played 7 and 8 September 2019 at Royal Liverpool Golf Club, Hoylake, Merseyside, England. Royal Liverpool hosted the 1983 Walker Cup. The United States won by 151⁄2 points to 101⁄2. They trailed 71⁄2 to 81⁄2 coming in to the final day singles but won 8 of the 10 matches to win convincingly.

Parmalat S.p.A. is a dairy and food corporation which is a subsidiary of French multinational company Lactalis. It was founded by Calisto Tanzi in 1961.

Having become the leading global company in the production of long-life milk using ultra-high-temperature processing, the company collapsed in 2003 with a €14bn ($20bn; £13bn) hole in its accounts in what remains Europe's biggest bankruptcy. Since 2011, it has been a subsidiary of French group Lactalis (with fuller control since 2019). Today, Parmalat is a company with a global presence, having operations in Europe, North America, South America, Australia, China, and South Africa.

Still specializing in UHT milk and milk derivatives (varieties of yogurt, cheese, butter, ice cream, etc.), the group also sells fruit juices distributed under the brand names Lactis, Santal, Malù, and Kyr. Its worldwide operations include almost 140 production centres and some 16,000 employees, with 5,000 Italian dairy farms supplying it. Its shares were listed on the Borsa Italiana.

YourTV (formerly TVCogeco and CogecoTV) is the brand of community channels owned by Cogeco. YourTV broadcasts into the Canadian provinces of Ontario and Quebec. Some channels broadcast in both the English and French languages, often on separate channels, in which case the French-language station is branded NousTV.

Programming the channels is produced with the assistance of volunteers. This programming was created in response to CRTC regulations which previously required that cable companies produce local content (this is no longer required but strongly encouraged)..

Salah Shahrour (Lang-ar; born 1 February 1988 in Aleppo, Syria) is a Syrian footballer who plays as a defender for Al-Nejmeh, which competes in the Lebanese Premier League the top division level in Lebanon and is currently a member of the Syria national football team.

Isometric projection is a method for visually representing three-dimensional objects in two dimensions in technical and engineering drawings. It is an axonometric projection in which the three coordinate axes appear equally foreshortened and the angle between any two of them is 120 degrees.

Lashkargāh (Lang-ps; Lang-fa), historically called Bost or Boost (بست، بوست), is a city in southwestern Afghanistan and the capital of Helmand Province. It is located in Lashkargah District, where the Arghandab River merges into the Helmand River. The city has a population of 201,546 as of 2006. Lashkargah is linked by major roads with Kandahar to the east, Zaranj on the border with Iran to the west, and Farah and Herat to the north-west. It is mostly very arid and desolate. However, farming does exist around the Helmand and Arghandab rivers. Bost Airport is located on the east bank of the Helmand River, five miles north of the junction of the Helmand and Arghandab rivers. Because of the trading hubs, it is Afghanistan's second largest city in size, after Kabul and before Kandahar.

After several weeks of fighting in the Battle of Lashkargah, the city was captured by the Taliban on 13 August 2021, becoming the fourteenth provincial capital to be seized by the Taliban as part of the wider 2021 Taliban offensive.
Wahyt Sähetmyradowiç Orazsähedow (born 26 January 1992) is a Turkmenistani football forward who plays for Turkmenistan.
Aberdare Girls' School was a state secondary school for girls aged 11–18 in the town of Aberdare, Rhondda Cynon Taf, Wales. It closed in July 2014.

Bader Philanthropies Inc. is a Milwaukee, Wisconsin based foundation that consists of funds from the Helen Daniels Bader Fund and the Isabel and Alfred Bader Fund. It pledges to give away $14 million annually. The organization centers on the health of older adults and improving lives of low-income Milwaukeeans and Jewish education in Milwaukee.

The Finse Tunnel (Lang-no) is a 10,300-metre-long (6.4 mi; 11,300 yd) railway tunnel west of the village of Finse in Eidfjord Municipality in Vestland county, Norway. The tunnel is part of the Bergen Line between the cities of Oslo and Bergen. It is the longest tunnel on the line, and the third longest railway tunnel in the kingdom. Inside the tunnel, at 1,237 metres (4,058 ft) elevation, is the highest point on the Norwegian railway network.

The tunnel was constructed to increase the regularity of the line past the Finse peak which was often blocked by snow. Planning started during the 1980s to remove this section that was regularly snowed down, and at the same time reduce the length and increase the speed of the railway. Construction started in 1990, and it was opened by King Harald V on 16 May 1993. Following the construction of the tunnel, 32 kilometres (20 mi) of railway around this area was also rebuilt to achieve higher train speeds, a shorter overall distance, and to maximize the natural protection from the weather. This was built in five stages, opening between 1995 and 1998.

Basharat Mirbabayeva (Lang-ru, Lang-uz; 5 December 1916 – 20 March 2010) was a locomotive operator in the Uzbek SSR. In addition to being the first woman in the Uzbek SSR to drive a train, she was also the first Uzbek woman to parachute from an airplane.

Milner is a city in Lamar County, Georgia, United States. The population was 610 at the 2010 census, up from 522 at the 2000 census.

Milner is part of the official Atlanta 29 County Metropolitan Statistical Area - MSA.

The Utría National Natural Park (Lang-es) is a national park in the Chocó Department, Colombia. It contains diverse flora and fauna in a lush, mountainous rainforest environment with some of the highest rainfall in the world, at up to 10,000 millimetres (390 in) annually. The park also protects the coastal marine environment, and is known for visits by humpback whales, who give birth in the lagoon after which the park is named, and sea turtles who nest on the beaches. There is accommodation for visitors, and ecotourism services are provided by the local indigenous people and members of the coastal Afro-Colombian communities.
Frederic Herbert Maugham, 1st Viscount Maugham, PC (20 October 1866 – 23 March 1958) was a British barrister and judge who was Lord Chancellor from March 1938 until September 1939.

Chenopodium fremontii is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae known by the common name Frémont's goosefoot. Both the species' specific epithet, and the common name derive from the 19th century western pioneer John C. Frémont.

It is native to much of the western half of North America from Canada through California to Mexico. It grows in many types of habitat from open desert, to shady forest, at 700–3,100 metres (2,300–10,200 ft).
Steve White (born 2 January 1959 in Chipping Sodbury) is a retired professional football forward and manager.
Nicholas Wadley (30 April 1935 - 1 November 2017) was a British art critic, art historian, biographer, cartoonist and illustrator.

Witch World is a science fantasy novel by American writer Andre Norton, published as a paperback original by Ace Books in 1963. It inaugurated the Witch World series and established a setting that she eventually shared with other writers.

The first hardcover edition was published by Gregg Press of Boston in 1977 as #1 in a set of seven called "The Witch World Novels of Andre Norton". Later it appeared in three-novel omnibus editions and in audiobook.

Charles-Joseph Natoire (3 March 1700 – 23 August 1777) was a French painter in the Rococo manner, a pupil of François Lemoyne and director of the French Academy in Rome, 1751–1775. Considered during his lifetime the equal of François Boucher, he played a prominent role in the artistic life of France.

He is remembered above all for the series of the History of Psyche for Germain Boffrand's oval salon de la Princesse in the Hôtel de Soubise, Paris, and for the tapestry cartoons for the series of the History of Don Quixote, woven at the Beauvais tapestry manufacture, most of which are present at the Château de Compiègne.

Celanese Corporation, formerly known as Hoechst Celanese, is an American technology and specialty materials company headquartered in Irving, Texas. A Fortune 500 corporation, the company is the world’s leading producer of acetic acid, producing about 1.95 million tonnes per year, representing approximately 25% of global production. Celanese is also the world's largest producer of vinyl acetate monomer (VAM).

Celanese operates 25 production plants and six research centers in 11 countries, mainly in North America, Europe, and Asia. The company owns and operates the world's three largest acetic acid plants: one in the Clear Lake area of Pasadena, Texas, one on Jurong Island in Singapore, and a third in Nanjing, China.

Give It Up is the second studio album by American musician Bonnie Raitt. Released in 1972 by Warner Bros. Records, Give It Up is an amalgamation of various genres, including folk, blues, R&B, and soft rock. Seven of the ten tracks on the album are covers, and the tracks are either soft sentimental ballads or fast-paced folk rock pieces. Lyrically, Give It Up revolves around Raitt's femininity, relationships, and being comfortable with herself. Raitt recorded the album at Bearsville Studios with producer Michael Cuscuna.

Give It Up was Raitt's first album to reach the Billboard Top LPs & Tapes chart, where it peaked at number 138. The initial reception was positive, as critics liked the vocals and the musicianship throughout the album. Contemporary reviews are also positive, with some critics calling Give It Up a stand-out album within Raitt's discography. In 2012, Rolling Stone ranked Give It Up at number 495 on its list of the 500 Greatest Albums of All Time.

Skimlinks is a content monetization platform for online publishers (including editorial sites, forums, bloggers, social networks, and app developers). It specializes in technology that automatically affiliates product links from publishers' commerce content.

The company was founded in London (2007) by Australian co-founders Alicia Navarro and Joe Stepniewski. It has over 62 employees, and has raised $24 million in funding as of August 2013. It has offices in London and New York City. On October 17, 2013, Skimlinks was announced as a member of Tech City UK's Future Fifty Programme, a government programme that supports fast-growing companies with the aim of potentially floating in the London Stock Exchange. In 2014, Skimlinks drove $625 million in sales for over 20,000 vendors.

Valmiera (pronunciation; Lang-de; Lang-pl see other names) is the largest city of the historical Vidzeme region, Latvia, with a total area of 19.35 square kilometres (7 square miles). As of 2002, Valmiera had a population of 27,323, and in 2020 – 24 879. It is a state city the seat of Valmiera Municipality.

Valmiera lies at the crossroads of several important roads, 107 kilometres (66 miles) to the north-east from Riga, the capital of Latvia, and 50 km (31 mi) south of the border with Estonia. Valmiera lies on both banks of the Gauja River.

David Jonathan Heyman (born 26 July 1961) is a British film producer and the founder of Heyday Films. Heyman secured the rights to the Harry Potter film series in 1999 and went on to produce all eight installments of the franchise. He also received three Academy Award nominations for his work on the films Gravity, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood, and Marriage Story.

Violet Dandridge, was the pseudonym for Serena Katherine Dandridge (1878–1956), she was an American scientific illustrator, painter, naturalist, and suffragist. She was the Smithsonian Institution’s first female scientific illustrator.

Walhalla-orden was a secret society founded in the early part of 1783 in the Sveaborg (today, in Finnish: Suomenlinna) fortress outside Helsinki, Finland by Johan Anders Jägerhorn along with Gustaf Adolf Reuterholm. It is thought to have been instrumental in setting in motion forces that eventually caused Finnish independence.

Laurette Spang-McCook (born May 16, 1951), credited as Laurette Spang, is an American television actress. She is best known for playing the character Cassiopeia on the original Battlestar Galactica (1978).

Spindizzy is an isometric computer game released for several 8-bit home computer formats in 1986 by Electric Dreams Software. It combines action and puzzle game elements. Players must navigate a series of screens to explore a landscape suspended in a three-dimensional space. Development was headed by Paul Shirley, who drew inspiration from Ultimate Play the Game games that feature an isometric projection.

The game was successful in the United Kingdom and was well received by the video game press. Reviewers praised its visuals and design, but criticized its audio. Similarities were drawn to Marble Madness, which was released in arcades two years earlier. Spindizzy was followed by a 1990s sequel titled Spindizzy Worlds.

Oxalis latifolia is a species of flowering plant in the woodsorrel family known by the common names garden pink-sorrel and broadleaf woodsorrel. It is native to Mexico and parts of Central and South America.

Deccani Architecture refers to the architectural styles developed during the Deccan sultanate period. The Deccan sultanates were five dynasties that ruled late medieval kingdoms, namely, Bijapur, Golkonda, Ahmadnagar, Bidar, and Berar in south-western India. The Deccan sultanates were located on the Deccan Plateau. Their architecture was a regional variant of Indo-Islamic architecture, heavily influenced by the styles of the Delhi Sultanate and later Mughal architecture, but sometimes also directly from Persia and Central Asia.

The rulers of five Deccan sultanates had a number of cultural contributions to their credit in the fields of art, music, literature and architecture. Deccan sultanates have constructed many grand and impregnable forts. Bidar and Golconda forts are classic example of military planning of Deccan sultanates. Apart from forts, they have constructed many tombs, mosques and madrasas. Gol Gumbaz (tomb of Mohammed Adil Shah), was the second largest dome in the world.

In 2014, UNESCO put a group of buildings on its "tentative list" to become a World Heritage Site under the name Monuments and Forts of the Deccan Sultanate (despite there being a number of different sultanates). These are:

Miniclip SA is a mobile game publisher and former browser game website that launched in 2001. It was started by Robert Small and Tihan Presbie with a budget of £40,000. In 2008, the company was valued at over £275 million.

In 2009, Miniclip hosted over 400 applications on its website.

In 2015, Tencent acquired majority stakes of Miniclip. In December 2016, Miniclip crossed 1 billion downloads across its mobile games on iOS, Android, and Windows devices. In March 2022, Miniclip announced that it had reached 4 billion downloads, with 8 Ball Pool alone accounting for 1 billion.

In April 2022, Miniclip announced that it would begin prioritizing its mobile games. As a result, the browser game portal was shut down in July 2022 and the website lost all but its two most popular games, Agar.io and 8 Ball Pool.

In June 2022, Miniclip acquired SYBO, the publisher of Subway Surfers, in an undisclosed deal.
The Council on Religion and the Homosexual was a San Francisco-based organization founded in 1964 for the purpose of joining homosexual activists and religious leaders.

Neodiprion abietis, commonly known as the balsam fir sawfly, is a species of insect in the family Diprionidae. It is found in North America from Canada to northern Mexico and is phytophagous, feeding on the needles of coniferous trees.
The Fine Arts Department (Lang-th, RTGS: Krom Sinlapakon) is a government department of Thailand, under the Ministry of Culture. Its mission is managing the country's cultural heritage.

The 2017 NHL Expansion Draft was an expansion draft conducted by the National Hockey League on June 18–20, 2017 to fill the roster of the league's expansion team for the 2017–18 season, the Vegas Golden Knights. The team's selections were announced on June 21 during the NHL Awards ceremony at T-Mobile Arena.

The Berlin Green Head is an ancient Egyptian statue head (AeMP 12500) made from greenschist and housed in the Egyptian Museum of Berlin, one floor down from the Nefertiti Bust in room 1.09. It has been considered the work of a highly skilled (though unknown) ancient Egyptian sculptor, as well as one of the most famous and credited pieces of art from the Late and Ptolemaic periods of ancient Egypt.
Abdul Qadeer Baloch (Lang-ur), better known as Mama Qadeer (Lang-ur) is a human rights activist from the Balochistan province of Pakistan.

Aquinas Walter Richard Sipe (December 11, 1932 – August 8, 2018) was an American Benedictine monk-priest for 18 years (1952–1970 at Saint John's Abbey, Collegeville, Minnesota), a psychotherapist and the author of six books about Catholicism, the clerical sexual abuse in the Catholic Church, and clerical celibacy.

Petro Pysarchuk (Lang-uk, born June 6, 1955, in Chemeryntsi, village, Peremyshliany Raion, Lviv Oblast, Ukrainian SSR) is a Ukrainian Politician and Entrepreneur. People's Deputy to the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine 4th, 5th and 6th convocations.

Philip Brandon Martin (born October 31, 1978), better known as Bishop Lamont, is an American rapper from Carson, California. He was signed to Dr. Dre's Aftermath Entertainment record label and released one project under the label, eventually leaving in 2010.

Nippu Ravva (transl. Fire Spark) is a 1993 Telugu-language action film, produced by M.V.Srinivas Prasad on Yuvarathna Arts banner and directed by A. Kodandarami Reddy. The film stars Nandamuri Balakrishna, Vijayashanti, and music composed by Bappi Lahiri & Raj–Koti with A. R. Rahman's background score. Nippu Ravva and Bangaru Bullodu, also starring Balakrishna, were released on the same day and both ran for over 100 days. It was dubbed into Tamil and Hindi as Suriya and Mawaali Raaj, respectively.

Barry Ono (1876–1941) was a British variety theatre performer, collector of penny dreadfuls and benefactor of the British Library. His collection of songbooks was bequeathed to the British Library in 1941.

TransPeshawar or Peshawar BRT is a bus rapid transit system in Peshawar, capital of Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP) province. TransPeshawar BRT system consists of two parts: the first encompasses an east–west corridor served by 32 stations on a dedicated lane for exclusive use by buses, while the second part consists of a network of feeder routes in which buses can enter and exit the system to travel on city streets. The system was inaugurated on August 13, 2020, and is the fourth BRT system in Pakistan.
Lucie Papineau (born April 26, 1962) is a writer living in Quebec, Canada who mainly writes children's books.

Clepsicosma is a genus of moths in the family Crambidae. As at 2022, this genus contains only one described species, Clepsicosma iridia, which is endemic to New Zealand. The species inhabits native forest in the North Island as well as the northern and western parts of the South Island down to Westland. The larval host of this species is assumed to be species of Cutty grass, possibly including Gahnia setifolia and Gahnia xanthocarpa, although the life history of this species is unknown. The adults of C. iridia are on the wing from December until May. They are nocturnal, and are attracted to light. During the day the adults rest on the underside of leaves, including those Cutty grass species that may possibly be their larval hosts.

"Rollin' in my 5.0" is a hip hop song written by American rapper Vanilla Ice. The song samples "Fly Like an Eagle" by the Steve Miller Band. The studio version of the song was only found on the single as it was originally intended to be featured on Mind Blowin. It is about Vanilla Ice's 5.0 Liter Foxbody Mustang (Ford Mustang (third generation)).

The kidnapping of Alexandra Măceșanu and Luiza Melencu happened in Caracal, Olt County, Romania on 25 July 2019. Romanian police were heavily criticized for taking 19 hours to locate and search the building where she was held captive. The handling of the case met widespread outrage and protests, which led to the dismissal of Interior Minister Nicolae Moga and other officials.
Civil Hospital, Aizawl is a state-owned hospital in Aizawl, Mizoram, India. Founded as a 'Coolie Dispensary' in Colonial India in 1896, it grew up to the form of a hospital in 1906.

Port Dock railway station – named Port Adelaide until 1916 – was located in the commercial centre of Port Adelaide, South Australia at the corner of St Vincent Street and Lipson Street. It was the original terminus of the railway between Adelaide and Port Adelaide, which opened in 1856.

After closure in 1981, the passenger station site was redeveloped as the Port Adelaide Police Station and Magistrates' Court. The former goods yard, adjacent to Lipson Street, is now occupied by the National Railway Museum. Several proposals have been advanced to build a new station, including a budgeted project by the government of South Australia in 2019, but as of 2021 none has proceeded to funding.

The siege of Ypres occurred between 8 June and 8 August 1383 as part of Despenser's Crusade and the Revolt of Ghent (1379–1385). It was conducted by English forces and forces from the Flemish city of Ghent. The siege was a failure.
Laura Rajnović Evetović (Serbian Cyrillic: Лаура Рајновић Еветовић; born 1 June 1975) is a politician and administrator in Serbia. She served in the Assembly of Vojvodina from 2012 to 2020.

Rappresentatione di anima et di corpo (Portrayal of the Soul and the Body) is a musical work by Emilio de' Cavalieri to a libretto by Agostino Manni (1548-1618). With it, Cavalieri regarded himself as the composer of the first opera or oratorio. Whether he was actually the first is subject to some academic debate, as is whether the work is better categorized as an opera or an oratorio. It was first performed in Rome in February 1600 in the Oratorio dei Filippini adjacent to the church of Santa Maria in Vallicella.

Molding sand, also known as foundry sand, is a sand that when moistened and compressed or oiled or heated tends to pack well and hold its shape. It is used in the process of sand casting for preparing the mould cavity.

The Show Must Go On (Korean: 우아한 세계; RR: Uahan segye; lit: Elegant World) is a 2007 South Korean action film written and directed by Han Jae-rim, starring Song Kang-ho in the lead role. The film revolves around a full-time gangster who's aspiring to be a full-time husband and dad.

The film released theatrically in South Korea on 5 April 2007.

Shaheed Lateef (11 June 1913 – 16 April 1967) was Hindi film director, writer, and producer. He was the maker of films like Ziddi (1948) which launched Dev Anand's career and Arzoo (1950) starring Dilip Kumar and Kamini Kaushal.
Luise Koch (11 October 1860 Bremen – 14 March 1934 in Bremen) was a German educator, politician (DDP) and women's rights activist.

Daniel J. Berthiaume (born January 26, 1966) is a Canadian former professional ice hockey goaltender who played six seasons in the National Hockey League (NHL) with the Winnipeg Jets, Minnesota North Stars, Los Angeles Kings, Boston Bruins, and Ottawa Senators.

The 2013 Kashiwa Reysol season was Kashiwa Reysol's 3rd season back in the J.League Division 1 since promotion in 2010, making it their 41st season in the top flight overall. They finished 10th in the league, won the J.League Cup and were knocked out of the Emperor's Cup and AFC Champions League at the fourth round Semifinals respectively.

Basuki Tjahaja Purnama (Chinese: 鐘萬學; pinyin: Zhōng Wànxué, Pha̍k-fa-sṳ: Chûng Van-ho̍k; born 29 June 1966) is an Indonesian businessman, politician, and former governor of Jakarta. He is also known by his Hakka Chinese nickname Ahok (Chinese: 阿學). He was the second minority governor of Jakarta with Chinese ancestry and also of the Evangelical faith, following Henk Ngantung, who was governor of Catholic faith from 1964–65.

Ahok was a legislator in the Indonesian People's Representative Council (DPR) and Regent of East Belitung. He was elected to the House of Representatives for the 2009–2014 term but resigned in 2012 to run for the deputy governorship of Jakarta, to which he was elected. In November 2014, he became governor of Jakarta, as his predecessor Joko Widodo had become president. Ahead of the 2017 Jakarta gubernatorial election, Ahok's political rivals aligned themselves with Islamic extremists to exploit religious and racial intolerance, resulting in Ahok being accused of blasphemy in October 2016. He then lost the election to former Education Minister Anies Baswedan and was imprisoned for blasphemy.

Wizzard Brew is the debut album by rock group Wizzard, released in 1973 on EMI's Harvest label. It reached a peak of No. 29 in the UK Albums Chart. In the United States, it was released by United Artists Records as Wizzard's Brew (with a different cover photo) but failed to chart there.

In 2003, Mojo magazine ranked it number 18 on its list of the "Top 50 Eccentric Albums".

TV Sound and Image, British Television, Film and Library Composers 1956-80 is a 2012 compilation album released by Soul Jazz Records. The album consists of a style of music known as Library music, music developed by production houses with the intent that the recordings would be licensed to various film, television, and radio programs.

Local councils (Hebrew: plural: מוֹעָצוֹת מְקוֹמִיּוֹת‎ Mo'atzot Mekomiot / singular: מוֹעָצָה מְקוֹמִית‎ Mo'atza Mekomit, Arabic: plural: مجالس محليّة Majalis Mahaleea / singular: مجلس محلّي Majlis Mahalee) are one of the three types of local government found in Israel, the other two being cities and regional councils. There are 124 local councils in Israel.

Local councils should not be confused with local committees, which are lower-level administrative entities.

In mathematics and computer programming, exponentiating by squaring is a general method for fast computation of large positive integer powers of a number, or more generally of an element of a semigroup, like a polynomial or a square matrix. Some variants are commonly referred to as square-and-multiply algorithms or binary exponentiation. These can be of quite general use, for example in modular arithmetic or powering of matrices. For semigroups for which additive notation is commonly used, like elliptic curves used in cryptography, this method is also referred to as double-and-add.

The Abbey of San Pietro al Monte is an small medieval monastic complex mostly dating to the 11th century, in Lombard Romanesque style, on a hill outside the town of Civate, province of Lecco, in northern Italy.

Founded in 796 as a small hermitage by the Lombard king Desiderius, the complex was considerably expanded in the 11th century, and then left alone in terms of major building and decorating. It was secularized in the aftermath of the French Revolution. Its 11th-century features remain very largely intact, including many frescos and carved reliefs.

In 2016 it was added to the "tentative" list to be a UNESCO World Heritage Site, as part of a group of eight Italian medieval Benedictine monasteries, representing "The cultural landscape of the Benedictine settlements in medieval Italy".
The Museo MAGI ‘900 is a private museum in Pieve di Cento, Emilia-Romagna, Italy.
Acadia Pharmaceuticals Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Sorrento Valley, San Diego, California.
HMS Ardent was a Royal Navy 27 knot torpedo boat destroyer ordered from John I Thornycroft & Company under the 1893 – 1894 Naval Estimates. She was the sixth ship to carry this name.
Dr Michael Spira (born 24 April 1944) is a British medical doctor and GP with a special interest in weight management.
Mouse-Warming is a 1952 Warner Bros. Looney Tunes short directed by Chuck Jones. The cartoon was released on September 8, 1952 and stars Claude Cat in his final solo cartoon.
Birdmonster is an American rock band based in San Francisco, California.

Myrica cerifera is a small evergreen tree or large shrub native to North and Central America and the Caribbean. Its common names include southern wax myrtle, southern bayberry, candleberry, bayberry tree, and tallow shrub. It sees uses both in the garden and for candlemaking, as well as a medicinal plant.

Tom Gorman (born January 19, 1946) is a retired ATP tour American tennis player and coach. He won 7 singles and 9 doubles titles and reached semi-finals in the 3 of the 4 ATP tour grand slam events. His ATP ranking peaked at 8 in 1973.

Tales of Zestiria the X (テイルズ オブ ゼスティリア ザ X, Teiruzu Obu Zesutiria Za Kurosu, pronounced "Tales of Zestiria the Cross") is a Japanese anime television series based on both Tales of Zestiria and Tales of Berseria video games developed by Bandai Namco Studios and tri-Crescendo, and published by Bandai Namco Entertainment. It is produced by Ufotable, directed by Haruo Sotozaki and written by Hikaru Kondo, featuring character designs by Akira Matsushima and music by Motoi Sakuraba and Go Shiina. The anime series is split into two halves: the first half premiered on July 3, 2016 and finished airing on September 25, 2016. The second half premiered on January 8, 2017 and finished airing on April 29, 2017.

The Regional Arts & Culture Council (RACC) is an organization that administers arts grants in Multnomah, Washington, and Clackamas Counties that also do advocacy in the Portland metropolitan area in Oregon, United States. It evolved from the city’s Metropolitan Arts Commission agency in the 1990s. In 1995, the Metropolitan Arts Commission became the RACC as an independent non-profit organization.

Vibal Publishing House, Inc. (also known as Vibal Group, Inc., Vibal, or VPHI) is a major publishing house in the Philippines based in Quezon City. Vibal was founded in 1953 by Hilarion P. Vibal and by his wife, Esther Asuncion-Vibal. Vibal is a textbook, reference materials, and multimedia products publisher and is the biggest in the country. In 2011, in partnership with Procter & Gamble, Vibal introduces digital classrooms and low-cost digital education tablets, for the digitization of education in the country.

Sadik Ahmad Turkistani is an ethnic Uyghur born and raised in Taif, Saudi Arabia and an opponent of the Taliban. Held by the Taliban in Kandahar prison in Afghanistan, he was briefly freed when they were overthrown in late 2001.

One of the Kandahar Five, he was taken into custody by the Americans and shipped to Guantanamo Bay detention camp, Cuba in early 2002, where he was treated as an enemy combatant. Finally he was cleared for release in late 2005. He was repatriated to Saudi Arabia on June 24, 2006.

Dardanella was a touring theatre company from the Dutch East Indies (modern-day Indonesia) established by Willy A. Piedro in 1926. Arising from a background of musical theatre, the troupe focused on realistic stories, both adaptations of foreign works and original stage plays about life in the Indies. Starring Dewi Dja' and Tan Tjeng Bok, the troupe performed original works by Piedro and Andjar Asmara. Popular both in the Indies and abroad, Dardanella dissolved during an international tour after 1936. Several of its members later went into film.

Paul Speratus (13 December 1484 – 12 August 1551) was a Swabian Catholic priest who became a Protestant preacher, reformer and hymn-writer. In 1523, he helped Martin Luther to create the First Lutheran hymnal, published in 1524 and called Achtliederbuch.

The Liberators'civil war (43–42 BC) was started by the Second Triumvirate to avenge Julius Caesar's assassination. The war was fought by the forces of Mark Antony and Octavian (the Second Triumvirate members) against the forces of Caesar's assassins, led by Marcus Junius Brutus and Gaius Cassius Longinus, also called the Liberatores. The latter were defeated by the Triumvirs at the Battle of Philippi in October 42 BC, and committed suicide. Brutus would also commit suicide after the second part of the battle.

Gracefield is a city in La Vallée-de-la-Gatineau Regional County Municipality in the Outaouais region of Quebec, Canada. It was reorganized on 13 March 2002 when three former municipalities (Gracefield, Northfield, and Wright) were merged into one.

Its name comes from Patrick Grace who opened there the first business and was mayor of Wright from 1885 to 1890.

Gracefield is located along the Gatineau River and Picanoc River. Its area is dotted with many lakes, most of which are lined with numerous summer camps and cottages.

Auguste Marie Fabre (5 February 1839 – 26 December 1922) was a French industrialist. He had utopian ideas and was involved in various cooperative experiments. He was the author of the 1896 booklet Les Sky Scratchers in which he extolled modern technological developments.

The C. H. Moore Homestead, in Clinton, Illinois, is one of two Registered Historic Places in DeWitt County. The other, the Magill House, was added to the Register in 2003. CH Moore Homestead DeWitt County Museum was added in 1979.
Andrea Batista Schlesinger (born October 27, 1976) is an American political writer and campaigner. She was until 2009 the executive director of the Drum Major Institute.

William W. Arnold (commonly "W. W."; August 28, 1843 – March 30, 1923) was a physician better known across the country for his avocation as an ornithologist. Born in Connersville, Indiana, he initially practiced medicine near Rushville, Indiana. In 1886, he visited Colorado and decided he would like to move there and he settled in Colorado Springs in October of that year. He operated a practice in downtown Colorado Springs and compounded medicine for his patients. He is best known as the founder and operator of a bird hospital, where he cared for ill and injured birds. He took care of domestic birds and had a large influx of wild birds after bad weather, like hail storms. He wrote articles about nature and birds, and he gave talks about them to school children in Colorado and Indiana. He worked as a physician and caregiver for the bird hospital until shortly before his death at age 79.
FOX Arena (formerly Arena) is an Australian general entertainment cable and satellite channel available on Foxtel, Austar, and Optus Television's subscription platforms.

The Walt Whitman House is a historic building in Camden, New Jersey, United States, which was the last residence of American poet Walt Whitman, in his declining years before his death. It is located at 330 Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Boulevard, known as Mickle St. during Whitman's time there.
HMS Penn was a P-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy during the Second World War.

Facebook, Inc. v. Duguid, 592 U.S. ___ (2021), was a United States Supreme Court case related to the definition and function of auto dialers under the Telephone Consumer Protection Act of 1991 (TCPA) to send unsolicited text messages. In a unanimous decision based on statutory interpretation of the TCPA, the Supreme Court ruled that auto dialers are defined by their function to either store or produce telephone numbers from a random or sequential number generator.

The 17th Arizona State Legislature, consisting of the Arizona State Senate and the Arizona House of Representatives, was constituted in Phoenix from January 1, 1945 to December 31, 1946, during the third of Sidney Preston Osborn's four consecutive terms as Governor of Arizona. The number of senators and house members remained constant at 19 and 58, respectively. The Democrats controlled one hundred percent of the senate. while the Republicans gained a single house seat.
William Patrick Twomey Sr. (14 May 1899 - 25 March 1977) was an Australian rules footballer who played for Collingwood and Hawthorn in the Victorian Football League (VFL).

Eblana (Lang-el) is an ancient Irish settlement that appears in the Geographia of Claudius Ptolemaeus (Ptolemy), the Greek astronomer and cartographer, around the year 140 AD. It was traditionally believed by scholars to refer to the same site as the modern city of Dublin. The 19th-century writer Louis Agassiz used Eblana as a Latin equivalent for Dublin. However, more recent scholarship favours the north County Dublin seaside village of Loughshinny due to its proximity to Drumanagh, an important trading site with strong links to Roman Britain; it has even been described as a bridgehead of a possible Roman invasion. However, there is no definitive proof to tie Eblana with any location, so its exact identity is still a matter of speculation.

The England Boxing National Amateur Championships previously known as the ABA Championships is the premier boxing tournament hosted annually by England Boxing. The Championships are 'open' class: any boxer who is registered with a club registered with England Boxing (aged over 17 years by the 1 October) can enter.
Oto Horvat (born 1967) is a Serbian poet and writer. He lives and works in Florence, Italy.
Lamakaan (Lang-ur) is an open cultural center in Banjara Hills, Hyderabad, India. The non-profit organisation provides space for events such as arts, literature, theater, music, and debates.

This is a comprehensive discography of Face to Face, a Southern California-based punk rock band. The band has released eleven full-length studio albums (counting a covers album), eight EPs, two split albums, one official live recording, one compilation album, eleven music videos and twelve singles. This list is not intended to include material performed by members or former members of Face to Face that was recorded with Legion of Doom, Me First and the Gimme Gimmes, No Use for a Name, The Offspring, Pulley, Saves the Day, 22 Jacks, Viva Death, Red Faced, or Real Space Noise.
Louis of Brienne, also known as Louis of Acre (died in 1297), was viscount of Beaumont-sur-Sarthe in Maine, France.

Tales of the Heroes: Twin Brave is an action game in the Tales series of games. published by Bandai Namco Games. It is considered a spinoff, and plays similarly to the Dynasty Warriors series. The game was released for the PlayStation Portable in Japan on February 23, 2012.

Latastia is a genus of lizards of the family Lacertidae. Species of this genus are distributed in Africa (Egypt, Ethiopia, Djibouti, Eritrea, Guinea-Bissau, Cameroon, Kenya, Malawi, Mali, Mozambique, Niger, Zambia, Senegal, Zimbabwe, Somalia, Sudan, South Sudan, Tanzania) but one subspecies (Latastia longicaudata andersonii) lives in Yemen. Collectively, they are known as long-tailed lizards.

Deryan Aktert, chairman of Dicle district for the Justice and Development Party (AKP), was shot by a group of seven or eight people at a gas station owned by him in Dicle on 10 October 2016, around 22:35 TSİ.

Wilson's snipe (Gallinago delicata) is a small, stocky shorebird. The genus name gallinago is New Latin for a woodcock or snipe from Latin gallina, "hen" and the suffix -ago, "resembling". The specific delicata is Latin for "dainty".

This species was considered to be a subspecies of the common snipe (G. gallinago) until 2003 when it was given its own species status, though not all authorities recognized this immediately. Wilson's snipe differs from the latter species in having a narrower white trailing edge to the wings, and eight pairs of tail feathers instead of the typical seven of the common snipe. Its common name commemorates the American ornithologist Alexander Wilson.
The Antifascist Committee of Ukraine is an anti-fascist and pro-Russian organization, closely linked to the Communist Party of Ukraine.

Yamada Amy (山田 詠美, Yamada Eimi) born February 8, 1959, is a popular but controversial contemporary Japanese writer who is most famous for her stories that address issues of sexuality, racism, and interracial love and marriage. Her debut and subsequent popular success in the 1990s was a part of Japan's hip-hop and Black culture boom. While she is most known for her stories of complicated and messy romantic love, she also writes on the daily minutiae of life (slice-of-life), child-raising, and bullying.

Major Edward Tempest Tunstall North JP (31 January 1900 – 1 January 1942) was a British Conservative Party politician. He was the Member of Parliament (MP) for Nuneaton in Warwickshire from 1931 until 1935. He was killed in action in 1942, aged 41.
Ladies Legend Pro-Wrestling, also known as LLPW and currently as LLPW-X, is a Japanese independent women's professional wrestling promotion founded in 1992 by Rumi Kazama and Shinobu Kandori.

Uyirmei is a Tamil medical soap opera that aired on Zee Thamizh. The series is apparently inspired by Grey's Anatomy and House. The plot revolves around the lives of some doctors, their lives, families and patients. The show premiered on 18 August 2014.First the director was Bhushan but after a couple of episodes Sundhar.K.Vijayan started to direct. Starting from Monday 24 November 2014, the show was shifted to 7pm time Slot. Its episodes ended on 30 January 2015 with 112 episodes.

Maris Harvey Taylor (June 5, 1876 – May 16, 1982), also known as M. Harvey Taylor or Harvey Taylor, was an American politician from Pennsylvania who served as a Republican member of the Pennsylvania Senate for the 15th district from 1941 to 1964 including as President pro tempore from 1945 to 1964. He was a political boss who held multiple political positions in Dauphin County and statewide.

The Church of Nossa Senhora do Rosário (Lang-pt) is a 16th-century Azorean temple in the civil parish of Nossa Senhora do Rosário, municipality of Calheta, being reconstructed after the Mandado de Deus earthquake that affected the islands of São Jorge and Pico.

Akumaa Mama Zimbi, also known professionally as Dr. Joyce Akumaa Dongotey-Padi, is a Ghanaian television and radio broadcast journalist, women's rights activist, blogger, marriage counselor, and an actress in Ghana. Akumaa is known for her fascinating, yet sexually stimulating, ways of expressing "Medaase" (Thank you). Her exceptional ways of wearing her headgear also comes off as a distinct Akumaa brand.
